message:
beads: normalize issue_type to 'task' across closed entries; mark wa-2l27x.8 blocked

Routine beads tracker metadata sync covering 18 records:

- 16 already-closed issues had their `issue_type` migrated from the
  legacy values `question` / `docs` / `test` to the canonical `task`
  value (8 from question, 8 from docs, 1 from test, 1 no-op refresh).
  This aligns the local store with the current beads_rust convention
  where non-task types are expressed via `labels` rather than
  `issue_type`:
    ft-0e179, ft-45805, ft-58uc0, ft-6269x, ft-6mxfs, ft-8wt4o,
    ft-gqu0a, ft-h8u5y, ft-kcsae, ft-l6v1r, ft-lnlg4, ft-n3mim,
    ft-qgk35, ft-trby3, ft-x2fvv, ft-x4k2b, ft-z01cl.

- Four of those entries (ft-58uc0, ft-8wt4o, ft-h8u5y -> `docs`;
  ft-gqu0a -> `test`) additionally gained a matching label so the
  original typing is preserved in the label dimension.

- wa-2l27x.8 transitioned from `deferred` to `blocked` to reflect
  that the underlying dependency has resurfaced and is now the
  active blocker rather than a punted item.

No content, description, dependency, or priority fields were changed.
Only `issue_type`, `labels`, `status`, and `updated_at` moved.
